programmes.show_description: A young woman claims her ex-fiance chose to support a child he knew might not be his and that she owes him nothing. S10, Ep146
A homeowner regrets hiring a trusted friend to housesit when her home is allegedly trashed, her pet abandoned and her alcohol consumed. S10, Ep147
A muzzled dog with a history of predatory behaviour is accused of attacking a Puggle at an animal control centre; a horrified owner is traumatised. S23, Ep110
A cyclist going the wrong way collides with a motorist who doesn't stop at a stop light; they both try to convince the Judge that they're not to blame. S23, Ep111
A stubborn teenager claims it was the colour of her skin not her poor driving skills that lead to complications after a dangerous wreck. S23, Ep112
A pet sitter claims her client's dog is terrified of him; the Judge orders an 'exchange plan' amenable to both parties. S23, Ep113
Two women fight over the same man, but one ex-girlfriend takes her jealousy a step too far after being accused of vandalism. S23, Ep114
A car travels between buyer and seller multiple times after the vehicle is allegedly repossessed before one final payment could be made. S23, Ep115
A young alarm technician says his boss paid to have his car repaired because he was such a profitable worker; his ex-boss disagrees. S23, Ep116
A childcare worker accuses a man of abusing his troubled grandson; he sues her for slander. S10, Ep141
A teenager sues her boyfriend for an alleged gambling debt, DUI fines and a pool set. Ex-lovers fight over gastric bypass surgery. S10, Ep142
The double homicide of a gay couple leads investigators into the twisted world of White Supremacists. Shell casings and analysis of glass shards help authorities find the suspect. S8, Ep17
Police discover a man shot dead in his home after they receive a call from his worried mother. The search for the killer would lead them on a two-year investigation spanning several states. S8, Ep18
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
When Devon Guzman is found with her throat cut, investigators think her girlfriend is to blame, however a jealous love triangle points to the killers. S1, Ep5
When a neighbourhood quarrel boils over, Billy Woodward decides to strike first. Billy crawls across the street and opens fire, killing two and injuring one. S1, Ep6
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
A Brooklyn woman turns to a N.Y. private eye for help tracking down a Russian beauty she believes robbed and killed her mother. Can they find her? Peter Van Sant investigates. S30, Ep5
Hot on the heels of a perpetrator, an investigator uses scientific knowledge to make an impression of his shoe print found in the snow. S6, Ep8
When an 18-year-old girl is abducted while talking on a pay phone, forensic evidence found on her clothing points police in the direction of a suspect. S17, Ep11
When a young woman is found dead in her bedroom, investigators use clever technology to lift a fingerprint from her pillowcase. S6, Ep9
A Los Angeles-based kidnapping, ransom and murder ring run by Russian immigrants is exposed when a pair of retired scientists use sonar equipment to locate its victims. S17, Ep12
After being diagnosed with Lyme disease, Neal Zumberge claims his neighbour's deer feeder is to blame. Their feud boils over into murder. S1, Ep7
When Linda Culbertson calls police about an invasion at her workplace, they assume she is a victim. However, police discover that Linda hired two men to stage the attack. S1, Ep8
48 Hours goes behind the scenes with investigators as they search for a mother of two who vanished just before Hurricane Harvey hit. Correspondent Maureen Maher investigates. S30, Ep6
When a woman dies after a year of nasty side effects to a drug, investigators had to determine if her death was due to natural causes, suicide, or murder. S8, Ep21
A woman is found dead in a ravine. Evidence had been washed away. Almost 20 years later, an old hat and a chip of stainless steel brought the killer to justice. S8, Ep22
After a girl disappeared from a town in Alaska, her body was discovered 10 days later; she'd been shot twice at close range, and sexually assaulted. S8, Ep23
Before a witness is to testify against the man accused of assaulting her, she is murdered. Investigators suspect her attacker, but don't have evidence to prove his guilt. S8, Ep24
A homeless mother is taken in by an old friend and throws a party while she is out of town; alleged property damage is quick to follow. S23, Ep117
Unwed parents of six are accused of owing a $5,000 electricity bill; the Judge uncovers evidence that the landlord's son illegally wired the home. S23, Ep118
A man borrows thousands from his own mother to help his girlfriend celebrate her big birthday; he expects to be paid back. S23, Ep119
The Judge clarifies to two young college freshman that pigs, not people, leave property in the state of filth they are accused of creating. S23, Ep120
A hotel negligently books two people in the same room which leads to accusations of jewellery and cash theft. S23, Ep121
A dog breeder makes every effort to appease a new Papillon owner but the threat of dehydration and holiday drama upset their plans. S23, Ep122
A woman buys over $600 in school clothes for her former employee's daughter who complains that she spent too much when asked for payback. S23, Ep123
A Hurricane Katrina volunteer comes back home to California only to discover the roommate she allegedly kicked out is still living there. S10, Ep148
A woman claims a roommate threw her and her two children out, kept her belongings and accused her of drug abuse. S10, Ep149
Former domestic partners dispute assault charges, a meddling mother and an abandoned cat. S10, Ep150
A woman escapes serious injury after her car is hit and flipped on its side by a driver in an allegedly stolen vehicle. S10, Ep151
Instead of repairing the BMWs he allegedly vandalized, a woman claims her ex-lover caused more car damage and broke her finger during a fight. S10, Ep152
A Naval officer claims he has a surveillance video to prove that his neighbour hit his car. A single mother of three demands payback from an ex-lover. S10, Ep153
A woman accuses her ex-husband of denying medical care to their six-year-old after the child falls and breaks her leg during a visit with him. S10, Ep154
A homeless mother is taken in by an old friend and throws a party while she is out of town; alleged property damage is quick to follow. S23, Ep117

A six year old girl disappears during a Sunday church service and investigators close in on a taxi driver found in the vicinity. S6, Ep10
Patients at the VA Hospital in Clarksburg keep dying after experiencing low blood sugar levels. An investigation into their deaths reveals an overnight nurse is to blame. S17, Ep13
When a beautiful model goes missing, suspicious photographs are found in a dumpster. S6, Ep11
A 70-year-old retired veteran is found dead on his boat after a bad sea storm. Investigators later discover he was killed during an attempted robbery. S17, Ep14
Couty Alexander calls police saying that he found his wife, Christa, shot. However, a gap in his story proves he is the one that pulled the trigger. S1, Ep9
When Sheila Bellush is discovered dead, police learn of a tumultuous custody battle with her ex-husband, Allen Blackthorn. But is his alibi water-tight? S1, Ep10
Was an Atlanta detective's investigation into an alleged rape and shooting death case influenced by TV cameras? Erin Moriarty investigates. S30, Ep7
A serial killer indicated he would kill twelve people, one for each astrological sign. Investigators got profile of the killer, and a stroke of luck enabled them to find him. S8, Ep25
A healthy man dies mysteriously in his sleep. Toxicology reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood. Investigators find a suicide note, but determine he didn't kill himself. S8, Ep26
In 1986, Kathleen Lipscomb's body was found on a deserted road. The crime was never solved, until two diary entries helped police to piece together what had happened. S8, Ep27
A retired police officer is discovered dead of a gunshot wound to the head. His wife says the motive was robbery; then she tells police he took his own life. S8, Ep28
A man not only asks a driver to pay for his ride but also to pay for he and his girlfriend's fast food dinner. S23, Ep124
Vicious pit bulls are caught on tape mauling a small poodle; a neighbour jumps over a fence to come to the rescue. S23, Ep125
A man, who denies being intoxicated, claims a car was trying to run him over when he busted the windshield in self-defence. S23, Ep126
A man admits that he is afraid of the Judge, who dresses him down for making poor business decisions; he said an online ad 'tricked' him. S23, Ep127
A man is grilled by the Judge when he allegedly threatens a woman's credit score; he claims it's all a money grab. S23, Ep128
A man admits to cheating on his lover while living at her parents' home, but claims only four of his phones contributed to her $4,800 bill. S23, Ep129
A man is accused of lighting his ex-fiancé's mother's car on fire and then hiding in the shrubbery; he denies all claims. S23, Ep130
The quarterback of an Arena II Football team denies owing a devoted female fan for a $2,500 car rental. S10, Ep155
A man denies taking advantage of a woman who was in love with him. A college student accuses drunk teenagers of stealing her camera at a party. S10, Ep157
A woman sues the grandparents of one of her children after the home she rented from them burned down. S10, Ep156
Angry ex-lovers dispute the arrest made after a violent argument. Neighbours sue each other after house-sitting and unsupervised children lead to damaged property. S10, Ep158
A woman claims her well-behaved son was bullied and beaten by another teenager. A teenager accuses his former mentor of taking advantage of him. S10, Ep159
